heading: Commanders of combatant commands: assignment; powers and duties
label: 164 Commanders of combatant commands: assignment; powers and duties

A, title V, § 518125 Stat. 1397Dec. 31, 2011“Whenever officers of the Armed Forces are considered for appointment to the position of Commander, Army North Command or Commander, Air Force North Command, fully qualified officers of the National Guard and the Reserves shall be considered for appointment to such position.”

Sense of Congress, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–181, div. A, title XVIII, § 1824(a)122 Stat. 501Jan. 28, 2008“It is the sense of Congress that, whenever officers of the Armed Forces are considered for promotion to the grade of lieutenant general, or vice admiral in the case of the Navy, on the active duty list, officers in the reserve components of the Armed Forces who are eligible for promotion to such grade should be considered for promotion to such grade.”

(a) of this section relating to assignment of commanders of combatant commands.Pub. L. 99–433, title II, § 214(b)100 Stat. 1018Oct. 1, 1986Oct. 1, 1990

children.label: A
children.text: has the joint specialty under ; andsection 661 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: has completed a full tour of duty in a joint duty assignment (as defined in ) as a general or flag officer.section 664(d) of this title
The President may waive paragraph (1) in the case of an officer if the President determines that such action is necessary in the national interest.

children.label: A
children.text: If the President removes an officer assigned under paragraph (1) to serve as the commander of a unified or specified combatant command or transfers that officer to another position or location before the expected end of the officer’s service as a combatant commander, the President shall, not later than five days after the removal or transfer takes effect, submit to Congress, including the congressional defense committees, notice that the officer is being removed or transferred and a statement of the reason for the removal or transfer.

children.label: B
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph prohibits a personnel action authorized by another provision of law.

The commander of a combatant command is responsible to the President and to the Secretary of Defense for the performance of missions assigned to that command by the President or by the Secretary with the approval of the President.

children.label: A
children.text: performs his duties under the authority, direction, and control of the Secretary of Defense; and

children.label: B
children.text: is directly responsible to the Secretary for the preparedness of the command to carry out missions assigned to the command.

children.label: A
children.text: To produce plans for the employment of the armed forces to execute national defense strategies and respond to significant military contingencies.

children.label: B
children.text: To take actions, as necessary, to deter conflict.

children.label: C
children.text: To command United States armed forces as directed by the Secretary and approved by the President.

children.label: A
children.text: giving authoritative direction to subordinate commands and forces necessary to carry out missions assigned to the command, including authoritative direction over all aspects of military operations, joint training, and logistics;

children.label: B
children.text: prescribing the chain of command to the commands and forces within the command;

children.label: C
children.text: organizing commands and forces within that command as he considers necessary to carry out missions assigned to the command;

children.label: D
children.text: employing forces within that command as he considers necessary to carry out missions assigned to the command;

children.label: E
children.text: assigning command functions to subordinate commanders;

children.label: F
children.text: coordinating and approving those aspects of administration and support (including control of resources and equipment, internal organization, and training) and discipline necessary to carry out missions assigned to the command; and

children.label: G
children.text: exercising the authority with respect to selecting subordinate commanders, selecting combatant command staff, suspending subordinates, and convening courts-martial, as provided in subsections (e), (f), and (g) of this section and , respectively.section 822(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall ensure that a commander of a combatant command has sufficient authority, direction, and control over the commands and forces assigned to the command to exercise effective command over those commands and forces. In carrying out this subparagraph, the Secretary shall consult with the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary shall periodically review and, after consultation with the Secretaries of the military departments, the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and the commander of the combatant command, assign authority to the commander of the combatant command for those aspects of administration and support that the Secretary considers necessary to carry out missions assigned to the command.

If a commander of a combatant command at any time considers his authority, direction, or control with respect to any of the commands or forces assigned to the command to be insufficient to command effectively, the commander shall promptly inform the Secretary of Defense.

commanders of commands and forces assigned to a combatant command are under the authority, direction, and control of, and are responsible to, the commander of the combatant command on all matters for which the commander of the combatant command has been assigned authority under subsection (c);

the commander of a command or force referred to in clause (1) shall communicate with other elements of the Department of Defense on any matter for which the commander of the combatant command has been assigned authority under subsection (c) in accordance with procedures, if any, established by the commander of the combatant command;

other elements of the Department of Defense shall communicate with the commander of a command or force referred to in clause (1) on any matter for which the commander of the combatant command has been assigned authority under subsection (c) in accordance with procedures, if any, established by the commander of the combatant command; and

if directed by the commander of the combatant command, the commander of a command or force referred to in clause (1) shall advise the commander of the combatant command of all communications to and from other elements of the Department of Defense on any matter for which the commander of the combatant command has not been assigned authority under subsection (c).

children.label: A
children.text: with the concurrence of the commander of the combatant command; and

children.label: B
children.text: in accordance with procedures established by the Secretary of Defense.

The Secretary of Defense may waive the requirement under paragraph (1) for the concurrence of the commander of a combatant command with regard to the assignment (or recommendation for assignment) of a particular officer if the Secretary of Defense determines that such action is in the national interest.

children.label: A
children.text: evaluate the duty performance of each commander of a command directly subordinate to the commander of such combatant command; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit the evaluation to the Secretary of the military department concerned and the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.

children.label: i
children.text: a qualified officer of a reserve component who is eligible for promotion to the grade of lieutenant general or, in the case of the Navy, vice admiral; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a qualified officer of the Space Force whose prior service includes service in a space force active status other than sustained duty and who is eligible for promotion to the grade of lieutenant general.

children.label: B
children.text: The requirement in subparagraph (A) does not apply when the officer serving as commander of the combatant command described in that subparagraph is (i) a reserve component officer, or (ii) an officer of the Space Force whose prior service includes service in a space force active status other than sustained duty.

Each unified and specified combatant command shall have a staff to assist the commander of the command in carrying out his responsibilities. Positions of responsibility on the combatant command staff shall be filled by officers from each of the armed forces having significant forces assigned to the command.

children.label: A
children.text: with the concurrence of the commander of such command; and

children.label: B
children.text: in accordance with procedures established by the Secretary of Defense.

The Secretary of Defense may waive the requirement under paragraph (2) for the concurrence of the commander of a combatant command with regard to the assignment (or recommendation for assignment) of a particular officer to serve on the staff of the combatant command if the Secretary of Defense determines that such action is in the national interest.

children.heading: .—Authority to Suspend Subordinates
children.label: g .—Authority to Suspend Subordinates
children.text: In accordance with procedures established by the Secretary of Defense, the commander of a combatant command may suspend from duty and recommend the reassignment of any officer assigned to such combatant command.

children.heading: .—Support to Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
children.label: h .—Support to Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
children.text: The commander of a combatant command shall provide such information to the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff as may be necessary for the Chairman to perform the duties of the Chairman under .section 153 of this title
